recycling_method: Clean the plastic, separate by type (PET, HDPE, etc.), shred it, melt and remold into pellets for new products.
tutorial_steps: Rinse plastic to remove food residue
Remove labels and caps if possible
Send to recycling center or plastic shredder
Melt and remold into pellets for reuse

recycling_method: Paper is soaked, pulped, filtered, pressed and dried into new sheets.
tutorial_steps: Soak in water to form pulp
Press and dry to create recycled sheets

recycling_method: Glass is crushed into cullet and melted to form new glass products.
tutorial_steps: Separate glass by color (clear, green, brown)
Mold into new bottles or jars

recycling_method: Metals are melted and recast into new metal products.
tutorial_steps: Recast into sheets or new products

recycling_method: Composting using microorganisms to produce nutrient-rich soil.
tutorial_steps: Mix with dry materials like leaves
Wait until it decomposes into compost

recycling_method: Specialized recycling centers recover metals and rare materials.
tutorial_steps: Take to certified e-waste recycling center
Recover precious metals and reusable parts

recycling_method: Textiles can be reused, donated, or shredded to create insulation or new fabric.
tutorial_steps: Reuse fibers for insulation or recycled fabric

recycling_method: Handled by hazardous waste facilities to neutralize or safely dispose.
tutorial_steps: Do not mix with regular trash
Follow proper neutralization or disposal procedures
